title: Podman fails to properly mount host /tmp directory on Macos
body:
### Issue Description

This was an issue opened on podman desktop repository, but we believe it is related to podman and hence opening it here. https://github.com/podman-desktop/podman-desktop/issues/13484

### Bug description

Podman fails to properly mount host /tmp directory on Macos. The pod commandline contains the mount parameter "-v /tmp:/tmp", the directory is mounted, but
not accessible from the pod. Listing the directory fails with "Permission Denied".

Expected behavior: 

Should see the content of the host `/tmp` directory. 
This way it works on Docker Desktop.

### Operating system

macOS Sequoia 15.5 M4

### Installation Method

Installer from website/GitHub releases

### podman desktop Version

1.20.2


### Additional context

Workaround

In MacOS `/tmp' is not a real directory, but a symlink to `/private/tmp`:

```
$ ls -dl /tmp
lrwxr-xr-x   1 root           wheel          11 2025-05-04 08:39 /tmp -> private/tmp
```
When I specify in the podman command line `-v /private/tmp:/tmp'
it works as expected.




### Steps to reproduce the issue

### Steps to reproduce

1. Install current version of podman 1.20.2 (cannot select this version from the dropdown menu above)for Mac on recent Macos/arm64 (15.5)  with default setting, just skipped installing the kubernetes tools and Compose).
2. Start Podman GUI and create a podman machine instance.
3. Run a container,  mounting Macos /tmp as /tmp in the container, expecting
to see contents of the MacOS host in the /tmp folder in the container:

```shell
 $ podman run -i -t -v "/tmp:/tmp" bash 
Resolving "bash" using unqualified-search registries (/etc/containers/registries.conf.d/999-podman-machine.conf)
Trying to pull docker.io/library/bash:latest...
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob sha256:e38e4d0798149331048b9dec4e0f6ecb73320d458dd5c07bb7668814b61c5883
Copying blob sha256:6f7899c8105dcba669fc98edab6c544ecaa2268a84aa04aacbd289d42424ad7d
Copying blob sha256:6e174226ea690ced550e5641249a412cdbefd2d09871f3e64ab52137a54ba606
Copying config sha256:799483b00dee2fda5d3cdcfb020fc2cf9652e3faaf65e8f112b9196dc66fb3cd
Writing manifest to image destination
bash-5.3# 
```

4. In the container try to list the `/tmp` directory

```shell
bash-5.3# ls /tmp
ls: can't open '/tmp': Permission denied
```
5. See that /tmp exists, is a directory and it's mode looks OK:

```shell
bash-5.3# ls -l -d /tmp
drwxrwxrwt   10 root     root           200 Aug  2 23:11 /tmp
```

### Podman in a container

Yes

### Privileged Or Rootless

None

### Upstream Latest Release

No

### Additional environment details

Additional environment details

### Additional information

Additional information like issue happens only occasionally or issue happens with a particular architecture or on a particular setting

comments.body:
So two things, first to mount a host directory into the machine VM they need to bet at machine init time with --volume, the default mounts are /Users, /private, /var/folders.
https://github.com/podman-container-tools/container-libs/blob/b17b0a4c55bb953de8d783ea26534df22fdc9f2b/common/pkg/config/default_darwin.go#L15-L22

podman run --volume then just mounts the volume from the VM to the container.

Since /tmp is symlink to /private/tmp and that is mounted it works which is expected. In general /tmp has a special meaning for most applications on linux so mounting over the real /tmp in the VM is forbidden because that should be a tmpfs and needs to support things like unix sockets which a virtiofs mount does not.
